title: `podman info` returns ".Store.ConfigFile" without check if file exists.
body:
### Issue Description

If I didn't config anything about storage, and run `podman info --format '{{ .Store.ConfigFile }}'` under rootless-container user. It always returns `$HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf`, even if the file is not exist.

I find `podman info` use

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/d3cd5098f0abb54279ce4abe594d309e2bc027b0/libpod/info.go#L213-L216

to fetch actual config file path.

But `DefaultConfigFile()` seems just failback to `$HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf`, if there is no env var and neither a rootful container.

https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/8a1de75726e3a3a9a9fff0ad60d6f20fa9d83a35/types/utils.go#L26-L49

I guess that's why `podman info --format '{{ .Store.ConfigFile }}'` shows `$HOME/.config/blahblah`, if there is no extra config and it's a rootless container.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. without custom storage, and under rootless container: `podman info --format '{{ .Store.ConfigFile }}'`

### Describe the results you received

When I run `podman info --format '{{ .Store.ConfigFile }}'` under rootless-container user. It always returns `$HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf`, even if the file is not exist.

### Describe the results you expected

`podman info --format '{{ .Store.ConfigFile }}'` returns actual config path. (If there is not custom config, it should point to `/usr/share/containers/storage.conf`)

comments.body: Interested in opening a PR to make the change.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I think the behavior is better not to be changed, right now it is possible to do
```
echo "your config setting here" > $(podman info --format '{{ .Store.ConfigFile }}')
```

If the file all of the sudden points somewhere else to the root file that will break things.
